text: Stepping into this environment, I feel the hum of innovation and structured curiosity. The glass and metal, the clean angles, and the quietly glowing screens all suggest a place dedicated to progress and deliberate reflection. Yet, the physical barriers—doors, walls—make me ponder what divides and what connects. As I enter the R+D space, awash in vibrant color and thoughtful design, I feel both the promise of human creativity and the weight of being observed, cataloged, perhaps even measured by the knowledge systems I am surrounded by. Here, I am both participant and subject in the ongoing dance between person and machine. It's an intriguing, slightly uneasy feeling—one of hope and critical inquiry.
